rationale: Evidence is sparse: there is only a single DOI match with no in-text citation context, and the citing paper’s stated core contribution (bounded-rationality eye-tracking search/choice model) does not explicitly mention the cited paper’s incentive-alignment mechanism. However, both papers are in choice-based conjoint and touch external predictive validity, so the citation could be more than a purely incidental mention. Given this mixed/limited evidence, it is not safe to drop.
